thread.title: Investigate thread detail sync
thread.last_turn_summary:
All of the old local app/server processes are down.

I killed the running `CodexMobileCompanion` app, its `bridge-server`, the attached Xco...

entries.summary: `thread_api.rs` is down to one lint nit now; the only remaining failures after that are in `codex_transport.rs`, which is unrelated pre-exi...

content.text: `thread_api.rs` is down to one lint nit now; the only remaining failures after that are in `codex_transport.rs`, which is unrelated pre-existing code. I’m fixing the last local nit, then I’ll let the targeted tests finish.

payload.delta: `thread_api.rs` is down to one lint nit now; the only remaining failures after that are in `codex_transport.rs`, which is unrelated pre-existing code. I’m fixing the last local nit, then I’ll let the targeted tests finish.

entries.summary: Edited files via apply_patch

payload.change:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/thread_api.rs
@@
     command
         .split_whitespace()
         .map(|segment| segment.trim_matches(|ch| ch == '"' || ch == '\'' || ch == '`'))
-        .filter(|segment| segment.contains('/') || segment.contains('.'))
-        .next_back()
+        .rfind(|segment| segment.contains('/') || segment.contains('.'))
         .and_then(|path| path.rsplit('/').next())
         .map(str::trim)
         .filter(|value| !value.is_empty())
*** End Patch
payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/thread_api.rs
@@
     command
         .split_whitespace()
         .map(|segment| segment.trim_matches(|ch| ch == '"' || ch == '\'' || ch == '`'))
-        .filter(|segment| segment.contains('/') || segment.contains('.'))
-        .next_back()
+        .rfind(|segment| segment.contains('/') || segment.contains('.'))
         .and_then(|path| path.rsplit('/').next())
         .map(str::trim)
         .filter(|value| !value.is_empty())
*** End Patch
payload.name: apply_patch

  4691	        };
  4692	
  4693	        service
  4694	            .sync_thread_from_upstream("thread-target")
  4695	            .expect("thread sync should fall back to archive");
  4696	
  4697	        let refreshed_target = service
  4698	            .thread_records
  4699	            .iter()
  4700	            .find(|thread| thread.id == "thread-target")
  4701	            .expect("target thread should remain present");
  4702	        assert_eq!(refreshed_target.headline, "Fresh target title");
  4703	        assert_eq!(refreshed_target.last_turn_summary, "Fresh target body.");
  4704	
  4705	        let untouched_other = service
  4706	            .thread_records
  4707	            .iter()
  4708	            .find(|thread| thread.id == "thread-other")
  4709	            .expect("other thread should remain present");
  4710	        assert_eq!(untouched_other.headline, "Unrelated thread");
  4711	        assert_eq!(untouched_other.last_turn_summary, "other summary");
  4712	
  4713	        let target_timeline = service
  4714	            .timeline_by_thread_id
  4715	            .get("thread-target")
  4716	            .expect("target timeline should exist");
  4717	        assert_eq!(target_timeline.len(), 1);
  4718	        assert_eq!(target_timeline[0].summary_text, "Fresh target body.");
  4719	
  4720	        let other_timeline = service

entries.summary:
The latency fix is in, and it keeps the current data quality path intact.

Opening `/threads/:id` and `/threads/:id/timeline` now refreshes...

content.text:
The latency fix is in, and it keeps the current data quality path intact.

Opening `/threads/:id` and `/threads/:id/timeline` now refreshes only that thread instead of rebuilding every thread snapshot first in [lib.rs#L1631](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/lib.rs#L1631) and [lib.rs#L1641](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/lib.rs#L1641). The new thread-scoped sync lives in [thread_api.rs#L167](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/thread_api.rs#L167), and it still uses the same RPC+archive merge strategy you now like for detail quality via [thread_api.rs#L1074](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/thread_api.rs#L1074) and [thread_api.rs#L1280](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/thread_api.rs#L1280). That means detail opens should be materially faster, but tool calls, file edits, terminal output, and archived-only events still come through the same merged timeline logic.

I added a regression test that proves only the requested thread is refreshed and unrelated cached threads stay untouched in [thread_api.rs#L4604](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/thread_api.rs#L4604). Verified with `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo check --workspace --all-targets`, and these targeted tests:
- `cargo test --manifest-path Cargo.toml sync_thread_from_upstream_refreshes_only_requested_thread`
- `cargo test --manifest-path Cargo.toml merge_thread_snapshots_supplements_rpc_with_archive_tool_events`
- `cargo test --manifest-path Cargo.toml thread_routes_are_available`
- `cargo test --manifest-path Cargo.toml thread_timeline_route_supports_before_and_limit_query`

`cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` still fails, but only from two pre-existing issues in [codex_transport.rs](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/codex_transport.rs), not from this change. Rebuild the macOS app and bridge, then thread-open latency should drop without losing the richer activity timeline.

payload.delta:
The latency fix is in, and it keeps the current data quality path intact.

Opening `/threads/:id` and `/threads/:id/timeline` now refreshes only that thread instead of rebuilding every thread snapshot first in [lib.rs#L1631](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/lib.rs#L1631) and [lib.rs#L1641](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/lib.rs#L1641). The new thread-scoped sync lives in [thread_api.rs#L167](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/thread_api.rs#L167), and it still uses the same RPC+archive merge strategy you now like for detail quality via [thread_api.rs#L1074](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/thread_api.rs#L1074) and [thread_api.rs#L1280](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/thread_api.rs#L1280). That means detail opens should be materially faster, but tool calls, file edits, terminal output, and archived-only events still come through the same merged timeline logic.

I added a regression test that proves only the requested thread is refreshed and unrelated cached threads stay untouched in [thread_api.rs#L4604](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/thread_api.rs#L4604). Verified with `cargo fmt --all`, `cargo check --workspace --all-targets`, and these targeted tests:
- `cargo test --manifest-path Cargo.toml sync_thread_from_upstream_refreshes_only_requested_thread`
- `cargo test --manifest-path Cargo.toml merge_thread_snapshots_supplements_rpc_with_archive_tool_events`
- `cargo test --manifest-path Cargo.toml thread_routes_are_available`
- `cargo test --manifest-path Cargo.toml thread_timeline_route_supports_before_and_limit_query`

`cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` still fails, but only from two pre-existing issues in [codex_transport.rs](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/codex_transport.rs), not from this change. Rebuild the macOS app and bridge, then thread-open latency should drop without losing the richer activity timeline.

content.text:
All of the old local app/server processes are down.

I killed the running `CodexMobileCompanion` app, its `bridge-server`, the attached Xcode `debugserver`, and the stale Dart DevTools processes. Verification is clean: `pgrep` finds no matching app, bridge, or debugserver process, and nothing is listening on port `3110`.

payload.delta:
All of the old local app/server processes are down.

I killed the running `CodexMobileCompanion` app, its `bridge-server`, the attached Xcode `debugserver`, and the stale Dart DevTools processes. Verification is clean: `pgrep` finds no matching app, bridge, or debugserver process, and nothing is listening on port `3110`.
